candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus shows conv=100% LONG but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0%, the signal is a structurally unbacked extreme — do not size up on consensus conviction without historical pattern confirmation.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: A 15/30 bull, 0/30 bear reading on USDJPY reflects current momentum agreement, but green_matches=0 means this exact indicator configuration has never resolved profitably in memory. Institutionally, a pair pinned at one-directional consensus right before a high-impact NFP print (per briefing) is overnight positioning that mean-reverts on the print — the consensus describes crowding, not edge.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus_conviction >= 0.90 and green_matches == 0 and hist_hr <= 0.10: size_multiplier *= 0.5  # untested extreme, halve exposure
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Reduces drawdown variance on false-conviction entries; expected +0.02 to +0.03 Sharpe by avoiding the largest single-trade losses on crowded pre-event positioning.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: USDJPY currently reads dir=LONG conv=100% with green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0% — maximal conviction with zero historical validation. AUDUSD by contrast shows conv=8% but hist_hr=72%, proving conviction and historical reliability are decoupled and that low-conviction/high-hr is the inverse-but-real edge.

retirement_flag.reason: L8 tightens the USDJPY/AUDNZD VIX gate to 13, but current state shows USDJPY at conv=100% LONG with no VIX context surfaced — if NFP-driven safe-haven flow is active, the gate may need re-validation against the new green_matches=0 regime; verify it still fires before this NFP cycle.

reflection.system_health_note: Regime is UNKNOWN with 0 trades in 7 days and TradingView providing no signals — system is correctly idle, sizing capped at 75%, but the USDJPY conv=100%/hist_hr=0% reading is a latent trap that should be gated before any pre-NFP entry.
